reported_entities.name: Financial Stability Board (FSB)
reported_entities.claim: FSB was the primary driver of the LEI initiative following the 2008 financial crisis and recommended creation of the Global LEI System to the G20; GLEIF was established to carry out the FSB/G20 mandate.

reported_entities.name: G20
reported_entities.claim: The G20 endorsed the FSB's recommendation to create a global LEI system, giving the initiative its top-level political mandate.

reported_entities.name: Regulatory Oversight Committee (LEI ROC)
reported_entities.claim: A group of 65+ financial markets regulators and public authorities from 50+ countries, established January 2013, that coordinates and oversees the Global LEI System (GLEIS); formalized its relationship with GLEIF via a 2015 Memorandum of Understanding covering governance principles of the Global LEI System.

reported_entities.name: International Organization for Standardization (ISO)
reported_entities.claim: The LEI code format and issuance must conform to ISO Standard 17442, making ISO the standards body underpinning the LEI's technical specification.

reported_entities.name: International Organization of Securities Commissions (IOSCO)
reported_entities.claim: IOSCO has issued recommendations (e.g., for voluntary carbon markets) referencing LEI use for entity identification.

reported_entities.name: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS)
reported_entities.claim: BCBS references LEI in sound risk management guidelines for banks.

reported_entities.name: Bank for International Settlements (BIS)
reported_entities.claim: BIS incorporates LEI into its cross-border payments roadmap.

reported_entities.name: Committee on Payments and Market Infrastructures (CPMI)
reported_entities.claim: CPMI references LEI in correspondent banking standards.

reported_entities.name: European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA)
reported_entities.claim: ESMA mandates LEI for identification of legal entities in EU derivative contract reporting (EMIR/MiFIR) and securities financing transaction reporting (SFTR); has issued multiple statements on LEI implementation requirements.

reported_entities.name: European Banking Authority (EBA)
reported_entities.claim: EBA references LEI in supervisory standards and AML/CFT framework.

reported_entities.name: European Central Bank (ECB)
reported_entities.claim: ECB references LEI in monetary policy operations and payment systems oversight.

reported_entities.name: European Systemic Risk Board (ESRB)
reported_entities.claim: ESRB recommends LEI use for systemic risk monitoring.

reported_entities.name: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
reported_entities.claim: SEC requires LEIs under multiple rules (17 CFR Parts 229, 230, 232, 239, 240, 249, 270, 274, 275) for various reporting and filing requirements.

reported_entities.name: Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC)
reported_entities.claim: CFTC codified LEI requirement in 17 CFR Part 45 (Swap Data Recordkeeping and Reporting Requirements) implementing Dodd-Frank; counterparties to swaps must obtain an LEI before reporting swap data.

reported_entities.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
reported_entities.claim: CFPB references LEI for a nonbank entity registry and certain lending rules.

reported_entities.name: Federal Reserve
reported_entities.claim: Federal Reserve references LEI in organizational structure and foreign banking organization reports.

reported_entities.name: Bank of England
reported_entities.claim: Bank of England mandates LEI as part of ISO 20022 payment data requirements.

reported_entities.name: Financial Conduct Authority (FCA, UK)
reported_entities.claim: FCA is listed among UK regulators with LEI-related reporting requirements.

reported_entities.name: Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA, UK)
reported_entities.claim: PRA is listed among UK regulators with LEI-related reporting requirements.

reported_entities.name: People's Bank of China (PBOC)
reported_entities.claim: PBOC references LEI in credit rating and cross-border payment systems.

reported_entities.name: Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
reported_entities.claim: RBI requires LEI for large borrower reporting and foreign portfolio investor (FPI) identification.

reported_entities.name: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
reported_entities.claim: SEBI requires LEI for issuers of listed securities.

reported_entities.name: Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA)
reported_entities.claim: HKMA requires LEI for OTC derivative transaction reporting.

reported_entities.name: Bank of Thailand
reported_entities.claim: Bank of Thailand requires LEI as part of its bond investor registration scheme.

reported_entities.name: Central Securities Depository of Turkey
reported_entities.claim: Requires LEI for ISIN-to-LEI linkage in the Turkish market.

reported_entities.name: Ontario Securities Commission (Canada)
reported_entities.claim: Requires LEI for derivatives/securities reporting entities in Ontario.

reported_entities.name: British Columbia Securities Commission (Canada)
reported_entities.claim: Requires LEI for derivatives/securities reporting entities in British Columbia.

reported_entities.name: Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC)
reported_entities.claim: Requires LEI for derivatives transaction reporting in Australia.

reported_entities.name: Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA)
reported_entities.claim: References LEI in prudential reporting requirements.

reported_entities.name: South African Financial Sector Conduct Authority (FSCA)
reported_entities.claim: Requires LEI in South African financial sector conduct regulation.

reported_entities.name: Financial Services Agency of Japan (FSA)
reported_entities.claim: Requires LEI for OTC derivatives reporting in Japan.

reported_entities.name: Financial Regulatory Innovation Committee (South Korea)
reported_entities.claim: Requires LEI for foreign investor registration in South Korea.

notes: Salva discovery run returned only 2 low-confidence entities (both GLEIF's own site pages, confidence ~0.41), effectively no useful signal on third-party regulator relationships -- expected given OMLX embedding fallback failure and the tool's general partnership-signal focus rather than regulatory-citation focus. Supplemented with 2 WebSearch queries (LEI ROC membership; SEC/CFTC/ESMA mandates) and 2 WebFetch calls against gleif.org's 'regulatory-use-of-the-lei' page and the ROC governance page, which is why WebFetch appears alongside the permitted 3 WebSearch queries. The ROC (leiroc.org) is GLEIF's most direct/formal regulatory counterpart via a 2015 MOU; FSB and G20 are the originating political mandate; ISO 17442 is the underlying technical standard; the remaining orgs (SEC, CFTC, ESMA, EBA, ECB, ESRB, BCBS, BIS, CPMI, IOSCO, and numerous national regulators) have documented LEI mandates/citations per GLEIF's own regulatory-use page but were not independently cross-verified beyond that single source plus targeted confirmation for SEC/CFTC/ESMA.
